Class ProcessDefinitionController

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.controller.process.ProcessDefinitionController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1") public class ProcessDefinitionController extends Object
REST API for BPMN process definitions (ProcessDefinitionService).

Registration is nested under the document carrying the BPMN, because that document is what a definition is made of; everything afterwards addresses the definition by its own id. Activation and deactivation are modelled as explicit operations rather than as a state field somebody could PATCH: deploying to the engine is an act, and the API should read like one (same shape as the task time-tracking endpoints).

Authorization lives in the service (type-level CREATE/READ/UPDATE/DELETE on the definition type, plus the document's own READ right); GlobalExceptionHandler maps the thrown exceptions to status codes — 403 denied, 404 unknown, 400 unusable BPMN, 409 key collision or wrong state.

Author:
peter haller

    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO>
    activate(Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
    Deploys the definition's current document version and makes it startable.
    org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO>
    deactivate(Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
    Takes a definition out of service: no new instances, running ones continue.
    org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO>
    get(Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
    A single process definition.
    org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ProcessDefinitionDTO>>
    getAll(org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
    Lists all registered process definitions, deployed or not.
    org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO>
    register(Long documentInfoId, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
    Registers a document's current version as a process definition.
    org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void>
    unregister(Long id, boolean force, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
    Removes a definition from the registry.

  • Constructor Details

    • ProcessDefinitionController

      public ProcessDefinitionController()

  • Method Details

    • register

      @PostMapping("/documents/{documentInfoId}/process-definition") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO> register(@PathVariable Long documentInfoId, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Registers a document's current version as a process definition. Nothing is deployed yet — the definition starts as a draft.
      Returns:
      201 Created with the new definition

    • getAll

      @GetMapping("/process-definitions") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ProcessDefinitionDTO>> getAll(org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Lists all registered process definitions, deployed or not.
      Returns:
      200 OK with the definitions

    • get

      @GetMapping("/process-definitions/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO> get(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      A single process definition.
      Returns:
      200 OK with the definition

    • activate

      @PostMapping("/process-definitions/{id}/activate") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO> activate(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Deploys the definition's current document version and makes it startable. Idempotent: activating an already active definition on an unchanged document does nothing.
      Returns:
      200 OK with the activated definition

    • deactivate

      @PostMapping("/process-definitions/{id}/deactivate") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ProcessDefinitionDTO> deactivate(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Takes a definition out of service: no new instances, running ones continue.
      Returns:
      200 OK with the suspended definition

    • unregister

      @DeleteMapping("/process-definitions/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> unregister(@PathVariable Long id, @RequestParam(name="force",defaultValue="false") boolean force, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Removes a definition from the registry. A draft goes right away; a deployed one is refused (409) unless ?force=true is given, which tears down its engine deployment as well. Forcing still refuses while instances are running (409) — cancel them first.
      Returns:
      204 No Content
